source_intake_record.raw_question_text: Given the block diagram of a closed-loop discrete control system as shown in <image>, determine \( Y(s) \) and \( Y(z) \).

choice_map.C:
\( Y(s) = \frac{R(s) G(s)}{1 + G(s)\left[H_1(s) + H_2(s)\right]} \)
\[
Y(z) = \frac{G(z) R(z)}{1 + H_1(z) G(z)}
\]

extraction_notes: image field contains a PIL image object placeholder, not an extractable path or URL
raw_answer_text was mapped from solution letter D to the corresponding choice text

image_dependency: question_text explicitly says 'Given the block diagram ... shown in the image'
image_manifest contains 1 existing PNG image and registry_passed=true
multi_step: task asks for both Y(s) and Y(z), implying two related derivations rather than a single lookup
answer_text contains nontrivial closed-loop expressions with combined feedback terms such as H1, H2, D(z), and z^-1
problem is based on a control-system block diagram, which typically requires combining multiple blocks/feedback paths from the image
verifiability: answer_present=true and answer_type is text/long_text with explicit symbolic formulas
choices are provided, so the final result can be checked against listed options
registry_passed=true with no asset issues reported
verifiability is slightly reduced because choices A and D appear identical, creating option ambiguity
